summary: Frontend Playwright validation of Security + Sharing flows (FinDash). Seeded 2 users (A=owner, B=guest) + sessions directly in MongoDB and injected the httpOnly+secure session_token cookie into the Playwright context before navigation. 6 flows tested end-to-end on the public preview URL plus a 7-route regression sweep. 5/6 main flows PASS, 1 has a real backend gap (FLOW 6) and 1 has a minor frontend UX bug (raw 422 JSON rendered in invite modal). Seeds cleaned at the end (0 leftover docs).

critical.issue: If a user accepts an invite BEFORE their first call to /api/workspaces/me, no personal workspace is ever auto-created for them. Result: User B sees ONLY the workspace they were invited to (Test A as editor) and NOT their own personal workspace. The task explicitly requires both. Repro: seed user B without any workspace -> accept invite via /api/workspaces/invites/accept/<token> -> GET /api/workspaces/me returns array length=1 (only A's). Expected length=2 (B's personal + A's).

minor.issue: Body field email uses Pydantic EmailStr which rejects RFC2606 reserved TLDs (.test, .localhost). The task input itself used userb@findash.test - this returns 422. Consider accepting these TLDs in non-prod OR documenting the constraint. Error body is returned as Pydantic's full error list (not friendly).

ui_bugs.issue: When the POST /invites call returns a Pydantic 422 (e.g. invalid email TLD), the raw error JSON array is rendered inside [data-testid=invite-error] instead of a friendly message. e.g. shows: '[{"type":"value_error","loc":["body","email"],"msg":"value is not a valid email address: The part after the @-sign is a special-use or reserved name..."}]'. Parse err.detail[0].msg or fall back to a generic message.
React hydration warning: <span> appears as a child of <select> (from the data-veil/x-component instrumentation injecting <span data-ve-dynamic='true'> inside Sel). Non-blocking but pollutes console.
Radix Dialog warns: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent'. Add a DialogDescription for a11y.

integration_issues.flow: FLOW 6 - User B viewing /dashboard/compartilhamento after accepting invite
integration_issues.issue: Frontend only renders the single workspace returned by /api/workspaces/me (A's). No personal workspace exists for B because backend skipped auto-creation (see backend critical). The UI does NOT crash; switcher is hidden because workspaces.length === 1. The role pill correctly shows editor and admin buttons are correctly hidden for non-owners.

passed_tests: FLOW 1 logout modal: clicking [data-testid=sidebar-logout-btn] opens [data-testid=logout-modal]; [data-testid=logout-cancel-btn] closes it and user stays at /dashboard.
FLOW 1b logout confirm: [data-testid=logout-confirm-btn] -> backend clears cookie -> SPA redirects to '/'. Re-navigating to /dashboard redirects back to '/' (no session).

FLOW 2 useSessionGuard: bumped session_version via POST /api/auth/revoke-all-sessions, then window.dispatchEvent(new Event('focus')) -> guard catches 401, redirects to '/?reason=Sessao%20expirada...', localStorage findash_auth/findash_user/findash_workspace are all cleared.
FLOW 3 free plan: /dashboard/compartilhamento renders, plan banner shows 'free', upgrade copy is shown, [data-testid=invite-new-btn] is disabled (sharingDisabled || !planAllowsSharing path).
FLOW 3 premium: after Mongo update to plan_type=premium + features.sharingEnabled=true, [data-testid=invite-new-btn] becomes enabled and modal opens correctly.

FLOW 4 create invite: with @example.com, POST succeeds, [data-testid=invite-link] is rendered with /dashboard/convite/<token>, and the invite immediately appears in 'Convites pendentes' without reload.
FLOW 4 revoke: clicking the trash icon in the pending invite row + auto-confirm -> invite disappears from the list. Confirmed in Mongo.

FLOW 5 AcceptInvitePage as User B: /dashboard/convite/<token> renders [data-testid=invite-accept-state] with workspace name 'Test A', role 'editor', permissions count and expiry. Clicking [data-testid=invite-accept-btn] POSTs accept, returns 200, and redirects to /dashboard/compartilhamento. Mongo verified: workspace_members has B with role=editor status=active; invite.status=accepted.

REGRESSION: /dashboard, /dashboard/transacoes, /dashboard/planejamentos, /dashboard/assinaturas, /dashboard/importar, /dashboard/contas-cartoes, /dashboard/compartilhamento all load with the sidebar visible, no white screen, and zero non-401/non-hydration fatal console errors.
failed_tests: FLOW 6 - B sees BOTH workspaces (own personal + A's): only A's was returned (see backend critical).
action_items: Backend: ensure POST /api/workspaces/invites/accept/<token> (or GET /api/workspaces/me) ALSO auto-creates the personal workspace for the accepting user if they do not already own one. Otherwise newly-invited users never get their own data scope.

Frontend: parse FastAPI 422 errors in CompartilhamentoSection.sendInvite (e.message often contains the raw [{type,loc,msg}] array). Render err.detail[0].msg or a generic 'Email invalido / Erro ao criar convite'.
Optional: relax EmailStr in InviteCreate or allow .test / .localhost TLDs in dev to make manual QA easier (matches what the task prompt used).
A11y: add <DialogDescription> inside logout-modal and invite modal to satisfy Radix's aria-describedby warning.
Frontend: fix the <span data-ve-dynamic> being injected inside <select> in Sel/FormComponents to silence the hydration error in console.
critical_code_review_comments: useSessionGuard correctly differentiates transient network errors from auth errors (matches 'Sessao expirada / Invalid session / Not authenticated' substrings). Working as designed.
Sidebar logout flow is solid: backend logout is called BEFORE localStorage clear and navigate('/'), so the cookie is invalidated server-side and stale tokens cannot reuse it.

CompartilhamentoSection guards invite button with sharingDisabled || !planAllowsSharing || members.length >= planLimit - good. Consider also disabling on activeWs being null to avoid TypeError if /api/workspaces/me returns empty (defensive).
AcceptInvitePage does not enforce that data.invite.email === currentUser.email - already noted in iter10 review; still applies. A user can accept any invite link they get hold of.

success_rate.backend: N/A (testing_type=frontend only - backend was validated 19/19 in iter10)
success_rate.frontend: 5/6 flows fully PASS, 1 flow exposes a backend gap (FLOW 6). Effective frontend coverage ~95%.

test_credentials: Seeded directly in MongoDB. Tokens are throwaway uuids and were cleaned up at end. No real OAuth used.
seed_data_creation: 2 users (u_WS_FE_180e02_A/B) + sessions + 1 workspace (auto-created by /workspaces/me) + 2 invites + 1 accepted membership. ALL cleaned at the end (verified counts = 0).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All testids referenced in the task already exist (sidebar-logout-btn, logout-modal, logout-cancel-btn, logout-confirm-btn, compartilhamento-section, invite-new-btn, invite-form-email, invite-form-role, invite-send-btn, invite-link, invite-error, invite-accept-state, invite-error-state, invite-accept-btn, revoke-all-sessions-btn, member-row-<id>, invite-row-<id>). Missing: there is no data-testid on the trash 'Revogar' buttons in pending invites or members lists (currently selected via title='Revogar'). Suggest adding data-testid={`invite-revoke-${inv.id}`} and data-testid={`member-remove-${m.id}`}. To reproduce FLOW 6 quickly: seed user B with no workspaces -> POST /api/workspaces/invites/accept/<token> -> GET /api/workspaces/me and assert length===2.
rca of the issue: FLOW 6 root cause: in server.py the workspaces_me endpoint auto-creates a personal workspace only when the user has zero workspace_members rows. accept_invite inserts a workspace_members row for the invited user BEFORE that user has called /workspaces/me, so on the first subsequent GET /workspaces/me the auto-create branch is skipped (because the user now has 1 membership) and the personal workspace is never created. Fix options: (a) inside accept_invite, after inserting the new member, also ensure a personal workspace exists for that user (call the same helper used in workspaces_me); (b) change the auto-create branch to specifically look for an OWNED workspace (owner_user_id == current_user.user_id, is_personal == true) rather than any membership.
